overall_summary: The ElizaOS ecosystem is focused on hardening core infrastructure and stabilizing multi-agent orchestration, while managing technical debt in plugin compatibility and addressing potential token market manipulation.
key_facts: PR #7197 successfully integrated '@elizaos/vault', a cross-platform secrets management system, into the core runtime on May 1.
The official Slack plugin was migrated into the monorepo via PR #7375, concluding the migration of major platform connectors.
PR #7376 implemented production cloud paths for monetized container apps and Cloudflare-managed domain support.
X (formerly Twitter) API integration now requires the official API, with direct login methods no longer supported.
The Hyperfy plugin was removed from the GitHub repository due to version compatibility issues with the elizaOS core.
Market analysis on May 8 identified a divergence between positive net fund flow and declining prices for ElizaOS coin, suggesting manipulation.
A critical Telegram integration bug was identified where dual polling between the core plugin and standalone wrappers caused 50% message loss.
Infrastructure hardening resolved critical headless Linux segfaults and race conditions in Telegram polling during the first week of May.
open_questions: How do I fix the movement issue in my chat application?
Is the X API required or if alternative login methods still work?
Why is the elizaos.github.io/summary/day page stuck since May 4th?

new_issues_prs.title: feat(vault): @elizaos/vault — cross-platform secrets vault + Settings UI integration
new_issues_prs.author: Dexploarer
new_issues_prs.significance: Adds encryption-at-rest for sensitive environment variables using OS-native keychains.

new_issues_prs.title: feat(slack): migrate plugin-slack into the monorepo
new_issues_prs.author: 2-A-M
new_issues_prs.significance: Consolidates the last major standalone connector into the primary monorepo.

new_issues_prs.title: telegram: milady wrapper + @elizaos/plugin-telegram both poll the same bot — race causes silent message loss
new_issues_prs.author: Sw4pIO
new_issues_prs.significance: Identified critical race condition causing random message drops in Telegram integrations.

overall_focus.claim: Emphasis on hardening core infrastructure, streamlining monorepo architecture, and scaling cloud-native capabilities including monetization.

discord_updates.summary: Discussions centered on the necessity of the X API for integrations, technical difficulties with the Hyperfy plugin removal, and manual distribution of plugin files as a workaround.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed frustration regarding the removal of the Hyperfy plugin from GitHub without clear documentation, and reported issues with the daily summary page being stalled.

strategic_insights.insight: The removal of the Hyperfy plugin due to version drift suggests a need for stricter versioning contracts between core and plugins to prevent 404 errors for developers.
implications_or_questions: Should the project implement a compatibility registry for third-party plugins?

market_analysis.observation: ElizaOS coin shows positive net fund flow while price action is declining.
market_analysis.relevance: Strong indicator of market manipulation or abnormal price discovery conditions.
